title: Fix /lab/evaluations account resolution to use AccountContext

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented fix and tests. Evaluations dashboard now uses AccountContext as the only account source (selectedAccount/isLoadingAccounts), removed ACCOUNT_KEY + listAccounts fetch logic, and keeps 'No account found' gated until account loading completes. Added component tests in dashboard/components/__tests__/evaluations-dashboard.test.tsx for loading/no-account/selected-account scenarios. Verified with: npm test -- --runInBand components/__tests__/evaluations-dashboard.test.tsx (Node 18.19.0 PATH-pinned).

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Addressed reload flash of 'No account found': evaluations now gates account error on auth state as well as account loading. Specifically, it only emits no-account when authStatus==='authenticated' AND isLoadingAccounts===false AND selectedAccount is null. Added regression test for pre-auth state so no transient error is shown during initial auth/configuring phase. Re-ran tests: components/__tests__/evaluations-dashboard.test.tsx (4/4 passing).

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Fixed remaining transient no-account flash on evaluations: when auth is authenticated and account loading is done but selectedAccount is temporarily null, dashboard now checks accounts list before showing error. 'No account found' is emitted only when accounts.length===0; if accounts exist and selection hydration lags, no error is shown. Added regression test for this exact state. Test suite now 5/5 passing: components/__tests__/evaluations-dashboard.test.tsx.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Found root cause: AccountProvider could transiently set isLoadingAccounts=false while auth was still transitioning, exposing empty accounts/selectedAccount and causing brief 'No account found' flashes in consumers. Fixed AccountContext fetchAccounts flow to keep loading true during authStatus='configuring' and explicitly set loading true before authenticated account fetch. Evaluations tests still pass (5/5).
